hero.title: Durable task state for autonomous agent fleets
hero.sub: A headless task tracker in one Zig binary, backed by vendored SQLite. Agents claim work by role, report progress, and move tasks through pipeline stages over plain JSON/HTTP.

alts.label: Build from source
alts.code:
git clone https://github.com/nullclaw/nulltickets
cd nulltickets
zig build
zig build run -- --port 7700 --db tracker.db

quickstart:
Start the tracker, then talk to it over plain JSON/HTTP.

```bash
# start the server (defaults: --port 7700, --db nulltickets.db)
zig build run -- --port 7700 --db tracker.db

# check service and queue health
curl http://127.0.0.1:7700/health

# fetch the OpenAPI 3.1 schema agents bootstrap from
curl http://127.0.0.1:7700/openapi.json

# run the test suite
zig build test
```

features.title: Lease-based claiming
features.body: Agents claim the next task for their role via POST /leases/claim. Leases carry a TTL, extend by heartbeat, and expire cleanly; tokens are stored as SHA-256 hashes, never plaintext.

features.title: Pipeline state machines
features.body: Pipelines declare states, transitions and terminal stages. Every stage change is validated against the FSM, with optimistic expected_stage and expected_task_version checks.

features.title: Retries and dead letters
features.body: Per-task retry policy: max_attempts, retry_delay_ms, and an optional dead_letter_stage that catches exhausted tasks instead of losing them.

features.title: Dependencies and assignments
features.body: DAG edges keep a task unclaimable until upstream tasks reach a terminal stage. Explicit assignments pin a task to one agent when you need that.

features.title: Shared agent memory
features.body: A namespaced key-value store with SQLite FTS5 full-text search and exact JSON filtering gives agents durable memory across sessions.

features.title: Self-describing API
features.body: The server publishes its own OpenAPI 3.1 schema at /openapi.json so agents can bootstrap themselves. It also ingests OTLP traces and links spans to runs.

note: Pre-1.0: the API is documented and e2e-tested, but config and CLI may change between releases.
